org_kg_contexts.name: Industry
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text:
Work with industry and the public to better identify use cases and address pain points using AI.
Most of the compute supply is expected to go toward industry use.
Singapore should continue to lean forward to support industry efforts to employ data for AI.
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
Industry enterprises may apply to the 100 Experiments programme.
Work with industry and the public to better identify use cases and address pain points using AI. This can be done through well-structured ideathons, hackathons, and incubator programmes.
Several companies, from a range of sectors, have participated in the 100E programme.
AISG helps pair companies with public researchers to develop AI solutions that address the former's problem statement, and provides co-funding of up to S$330,000 per project.
100 Experiments (100E) is AISG's flagship programme, aimed at solving AI problem statements proposed by industry.
100 Experiments (100E) is AISG's flagship programme, aimed at solving AI problem statements proposed by industry where no commercial off-the-shelf AI solutions exist.
Through Company-Led Training Programmes and Career Conversion Programmes, amongst others, TeSA has partnered industry to train individuals in AI and Data Analytics.
The AIAP is currently designed as a 9-month, full-time, deep-skilling company-led training programme, that helps trainees acquire AI capabilities by working alongside industry teams on real business problems.
TeSA has partnered industry to train individuals in AI and Data Analytics.
Through programmes like SMEs Go Digital and CTO-as-a-Service (CTOaaS), the Government has helped businesses gain awareness of suitable AI-enabled solutions.
A trusted environment where AI-enabled innovations and systems are robust and safe, so that our people can engage with AI with confidence.

org_kg_contexts.name: Enterprise
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text:
Enterprises are end-users and demand drivers for AI solutions.
Recent advances, especially in Generative AI, have opened doors to new possibilities for enterprises to adopt AI.
We must invest in our people, enterprises, and digital infrastructure.
This requires investments in our people, enterprises, and digital infrastructure, so that we can create new jobs and participate in industries of the future.
We shall uplift and empower our people and businesses, equipping them with the capabilities and resources to thrive in an AI-enabled future.
A confident base of enterprises and workers can use AI tools.
A confident base of enterprises and workers can use AI solutions.
Industry, Government, and Research drive AI activity, from basic science to product R&D and adoption.
Businesses were given free access to Google Cloud's AI toolsets for up to three months.
Many companies have already embarked on AI-enabled innovation projects, which are often focused on the Application layer.
We will work with Industry Champions to identify sector use cases and crowd in a broader base of researchers and companies in the sector.
Research breakthroughs can translate into commercial products, which can be used by our companies.
Their research breakthroughs can also translate into commercial products, which can be used by our companies and across government agencies to realise better outcomes.
100 Experiments (100E) is AISG's flagship programme, aimed at solving AI problem statements proposed by industry where no commercial off-the-shelf AI solutions exist.
Several companies, from a range of sectors, have participated in the 100E programme.
The project also drew in industry partners from both countries.
AI has the potential to help enterprises increase their productivity and stay relevant, provided they have the capabilities to apply it well.
Empower enterprises and workers to adopt AI and stay ahead of the curve.
Singapore will intensify the promotion of AI adoption across all enterprises.
We will promote baseline digital adoption for our enterprises and enhance the enterprise digitalisation toolkit, to support more sophisticated AI adoption.
For more digitally mature enterprises, we will provide tailored support for AI-enabled business transformation.
The vibrancy of the community in turn attracts the participation of even more talented individuals, companies, and capital.
The data concierge can explore facilitating handshakes between private industry entities as a trusted intermediary.
The Government will also elevate the security and resilience baseline for all system owners using AI.
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
AI Trailblazers provides free access to Google Cloud AI toolsets for up to three months.
The AI Trailblazers programme provides free access to Google Cloud AI toolsets for up to three months to generate 100 Generative AI use cases in 100 days.
More of our technology workforce should work to scale novel AI solutions, that form part of the toolkit which a confident base of enterprises and workers can use.
Foundational AI, where scientific advancements are still needed to improve AI’s abilities. For a start, we will double down on three specific themes – Reasoning AI, Resource-Efficient AI, and Responsible AI – with a view to making AI less costly, more widely-used, and most importantly, trusted.
For a start, we will double down on three specific themes – Reasoning AI, Resource-Efficient AI, and Responsible AI – with a view to making AI less costly, more widely-used, and most importantly, trusted.
Their research breakthroughs can also translate into commercial products, which can be used by our companies and across government agencies to realise better outcomes.
Workers need to utilise AI tools, so that they can increase their productivity, avoid untimely disruptions to their jobs, and help enterprises optimise their operations and improve their competitiveness.
We have made available tools that enterprises can use to evaluate their readiness to adopt AI. For example, self-assessment tools like AISG's AI Readiness Index (AIRI) are useful in helping companies identify and map out the capabilities and infrastructure they need for using AI.
This includes leveraging IMDA and EnterpriseSG’s Digital Leaders Programme (DLP), which helps companies build in-house digital capabilities, as well as the scoping of impactful AI projects in partnership with AI solution providers.
the vibrancy of the community in turn attracts the participation of even more talented individuals, companies, and capital.
Singapore-based company that participates in the Digital Leaders Programme to build in-house digital capabilities and scope AI projects.
AI Singapore evaluates project outcomes and impact.
For private sector datasets, the Government can consider stepping in for selected high-value use cases where we can play a catalytic role.

org_kg_contexts.name: Singapore Government
org_kg_contexts.role_scope: Lead AI adoption in Smart Nation priorities and issue policy guidelines
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text:
Singapore believes in the transformative potential of AI. Our second National AI Strategy represents Singapore's commitment to realise the benefits of AI.
The report sets out government plans to build a thriving AI ecosystem, develop workforce, provide infrastructure, and foster a trusted environment.
In Singapore, where the vast majority of our people already frequently interact with digital technologies to transact with the Government and businesses.
Singapore believes that AI can be a potent force for good, to uplift human potential.
The recent breakthroughs in AI demand a renewed focus by Singapore to refine our national strategies for AI.
Singapore's second National AI Strategy (NAIS 2.0) will drive the next bound of our Smart Nation journey.
NAIS 2.0 starts with the conviction that we must do our utmost to harness AI for the Public Good, for Singapore and the World.
Singapore intends to provide more platforms which can bring our AI community together.
Singapore must ensure reliable, localised access to high-performance compute, so that industry, academia, and the Government have the means to innovate and build here.
Singapore is determined to play our part, and we will continue to participate constructively at international fora on AI.
Singapore is committed to being a serious and reliable international partner on AI.
Singapore demonstrates commitment to developing and deploying AI well, and will leverage these gains to be a global pace-setter.
We will actively profile Singapore's approaches to AI through public engagements and conferences such as Asia Tech x Singapore and SCAI.
Singapore’s NAIS 2.0 is therefore designed to unfold as a national movement on the global stage.
Published 4 December 2023 Copyright © 2023 Government of the Republic of Singapore
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
This report spells out how we will achieve our vision.
It sets out our plans to build a thriving AI ecosystem
Singapore believes that AI can be a potent force for good, to uplift human potential.
This requires us to steer AI for the Public Good.
AI is strategically important. We must invest in it to unlock the next frontier of economic growth.
Address risks from the potential abuse and mismanagement of AI. These include the misuse of AI to amplify harms (e.g. scams, cyberattacks, and mis/disinformation) across society, especially toward vulnerable groups.
We will take a systems approach, bringing together stakeholders within and outside Singapore to add to our resources, capabilities, and infrastructure, accelerate the exchange of ideas, and administer AI-enabled solutions at scale.
Singapore must contribute to AI breakthroughs and products that the world values.
We will continually review these Actions to respond to fast-moving developments in AI, across domains.
We will undertake across these systems and enablers, to support our ambitions over the next 3-5 years.
We will develop and roll out targeted courses to impart AI skills, for different public sector workforce segments (e.g. senior management, policy, and operational roles).
This could take the form of joint grant calls or PhD training programmes with other countries, or involvement in international conferences.
Through programmes like SMEs Go Digital and CTO-as-a-Service (CTOaaS), the Government has helped businesses gain awareness of suitable AI-enabled solutions.
we should invest in Privacy-Enhancing Technologies (PETs), and other novel approaches that can address barriers around data protection and sharing, to encourage more data flows.
The Government provides central funding to support AI use cases in the public sector.
Singapore is determined to play our part, and we will continue to participate constructively at international fora on AI.

org_kg_contexts.name: Singapore
org_kg_contexts.role_scope: Lead AI adoption in Smart Nation priorities
org_kg_contexts.source_spans_text: Singapore aspires to be a pace-setter – a global leader in choice AI areas, that are economically impactful and serve the Public Good.
org_kg_contexts.edge_source:
We will participate fully in AI research and deployment.
Singapore aspires to be a pace-setter – a global leader in choice AI areas, that are economically impactful and serve the Public Good.
Singapore must contribute to AI breakthroughs and products that the world values.
This strategy statement outlines 15 Actions that Singapore will undertake across these systems and enablers, to support our ambitions over the next 3-5 years.
We will continually review these Actions to respond to fast-moving developments in AI, across domains.
We will ensure that Singapore hosts the necessary infrastructure and provides a trusted environment for AI innovation.
We hope to participate in shaping the international rules of the road that are emerging around AI.
Safeguarding Singapore’s AI interests in the international arena. We hope to participate in shaping the international rules of the road that are emerging around AI.
It remains critical for Singapore to stay engaged internationally, in a multi-party, multi-stakeholder fashion, to work towards a common understanding on AI.
We will continue to participate constructively at international fora on AI.
Singapore is committed to being a serious and reliable international partner on AI.
Continue to grow Singapore’s international networks with key partner countries and leading AI companies.
Singapore will support and actively participate in substantive multilateral, multi-stakeholder, or plurilateral initiatives, that seek to achieve an inclusive, practical, and rules-based global environment for AI.
Singapore will support and actively participate in substantive multilateral, multi-stakeholder, or plurilateral initiatives.
Anchoring key bilateral relationships with selected partners from government and industry, through substantive initiatives and technical cooperation.
actively participate in substantive multilateral, multi-stakeholder, or plurilateral initiatives, that seek to achieve an inclusive, practical, and rules-based global environment for AI.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Chua is a veteran AI researcher who moved from academia to lead a corporate research lab in a large Singapore bank. He focuses on applying NLP to automate compliance and risk assessment. He serves on the advisory board of AI Singapore and champions the adoption of AI in the financial sector.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in natural language processing, named entity recognition, and document understanding for the financial domain. Experienced in managing large-scale AI projects with stringent regulatory requirements.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to deploy Singapore's first fully automated compliance assistant for banking, reducing manual review time by 80%. He aims to influence the national AI strategy to prioritize domain-specific foundational models for finance.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-jyskh2mw
participants.professional_persona: Early-career researcher in a university AI lab working on resource-efficient AI models. She participated in a joint grant call with an industry partner to develop low-cost AI solutions for smallholder farmers in Southeast Asia, aligning with the Public Good principle.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Strong background in machine learning model compression and federated learning, with publications on reducing compute cost. Proficient in translating research into pilot prototypes that can be tested by industry partners under the 100 Experiments scheme.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aspires to become a principal investigator leading a lab focused on AI for social impact, and to contribute to Singapore's goal of being a global pace-setter in choice AI areas. Believes that steering AI for Public Good means ensuring breakthroughs translate into affordable, accessible products.

participants.professional_persona: A research scientist from A*STAR who participates in 100 Experiments projects, pairing with companies to develop bespoke AI solutions. He specialises in resource-efficient AI to make models deployable on low-power devices.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in model compression, knowledge distillation, and hardware-software co-design. Publishes at top-tier AI conferences.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Hopes to spin off a deep-tech startup from his research and contribute to Singapore's leadership in resource-efficient AI.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-lv1a3ydr
participants.professional_persona: Senior product manager at a fintech startup that recently completed the Digital Leaders Programme to scope an AI-driven fraud detection project. She actively participates in industry roundtables on AI adoption and mentors junior professionals through TeSA's Career Conversion Programme.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expertise in productising AI solutions for financial services, including credit scoring and anomaly detection. Skilled at bridging the gap between technical teams and business stakeholders, and experienced in applying self-assessment tools like the AI Readiness Index.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to lead the AI transformation of her startup into a regional leader in ethical fintech, and later advise government on policies that enable enterprises to adopt AI responsibly. Views the focal principle as a guide to ensure AI solutions enhance jobs and build public trust.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Lim is a senior research scientist at A*STAR's Centre for AI, specialising in resource-efficient deep learning. She collaborates with manufacturing SMEs to deploy lightweight computer vision models on edge devices.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in model compression, quantization, and knowledge distillation; experienced in deploying AI on low-resource hardware for industrial inspection.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: She aims to make AI affordable for small factories in Singapore, and eventually spin out a startup offering cost-effective AI solutions.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-bmugmys6
participants.professional_persona: Mid-career professional with 12 years in logistics and supply chain management. Currently exploring AI to optimize route planning and reduce carbon footprint for a regional distribution firm.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Lean Six Sigma Green Belt, SAP ERP proficiency, and basic Python scripting for data analysis. Recently completed an AI for Business online certificate.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aim to lead a digital transformation team in logistics, deploying AI solutions that cut operational waste and improve delivery reliability across Southeast Asia.

participants.professional_persona: A polytechnic graduate who completed a Specialist Diploma in AI and now works as an AI technician at a manufacturing firm. He fine-tunes computer vision models for quality inspection on the production line.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Hands-on with image annotation, model retraining, and edge deployment. Strong troubleshooting skills in a factory environment.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to progress to a full AI engineer role and eventually lead the digital transformation team at his company.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Tan recently returned to Singapore after a postdoc at MIT, focusing on reasoning AI and logical inference in neural networks. He is now a tenure-track assistant professor at NUS.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in neuro-symbolic reasoning, automated theorem proving, and transformer architecture modifications for enhanced reasoning.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He wants to establish a world-class research group in reasoning AI and contribute to Singapore's leadership in trusted AI systems.

participants.professional_persona: A returning mother who re-entered the workforce through a TeSA training programme. She now works as an AI operations engineer at a logistics company, maintaining and monitoring deployed models for route optimisation.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Skilled in MLOps, model monitoring, and DevOps pipelines. Brings strong project coordination and communication skills from her previous career in operations management.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to become a senior MLOps engineer and champion flexible work arrangements for women re-entering tech.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-osql3pol
participants.professional_persona: Recent computer science graduate who built a chatbot for a community centre during university. Passionate about using AI to improve public services for seniors and low-income families.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Full-stack web development, natural language processing with Hugging Face, and user research methods. Has contributed to two open-source AI projects.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seek a role where I can design inclusive AI tools that bridge digital divides, and eventually found a social enterprise focused on AI for community well-being.

participants.professional_persona: A seasoned AI engineer who transitioned from a traditional software role after completing the AI Apprenticeship Programme (AIAP). Now works at a large logistics firm, deploying predictive models to optimise supply chain operations.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Proficient in Python, TensorFlow, and cloud-based MLOps pipelines; experienced in applying computer vision for warehouse automation.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to lead a team of AI engineers within three years and contribute to Singapore's Smart Nation initiatives by building scalable, trusted AI systems for the logistics sector.

participants.professional_persona: Ms. Goh is a lead AI engineer at a fintech startup, developing fraud detection models using graph neural networks. She previously worked at a large bank and pivoted to applied research.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Skilled in graph neural networks, anomaly detection, and scalable ML pipelines; experienced in translating research into production systems.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: She hopes to publish her work at top-tier conferences and eventually move into a head of research role where she can shape company AI strategy.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-cpebn104
participants.professional_persona: Experienced educator and curriculum designer at a polytechnic, integrating AI literacy into business courses. Believes AI should empower workers rather than displace them.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Instructional design, stakeholder engagement, and familiarity with no-code AI platforms. Has run two adult-training workshops on AI fundamentals.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Develop a national-level AI upskilling programme for mid-career professionals, ensuring inclusive access to emerging technology training.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-tiip0byr
participants.professional_persona: Serial entrepreneur in the food & beverage sector, having launched three cloud kitchens. Now exploring AI-driven demand forecasting and waste reduction.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Business strategy, vendor negotiation, and basic analytics using Excel and Tableau. Participated in a government SME digitalisation workshop.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Scale my F&B group by adopting AI inventory management, and mentor other SME owners on practical AI adoption journeys.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-kgwws4qf
participants.professional_persona: Healthcare administrator at a public hospital, responsible for patient flow and resource allocation. Keen to implement AI triage tools to reduce waiting times.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Healthcare operations management, data governance, and project coordination. Completed a short course on AI in Healthcare from NUS.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Drive evidence-based AI adoption in public healthcare to improve patient outcomes, and later contribute to national health AI policy.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Wong is a principal researcher at a government-funded AI lab working on generative AI for public communications. She helps agencies generate multilingual content responsibly.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in large language model fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and culturally-aware text generation; published papers on responsible AI.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: She aims to develop open-source models that are safe for government use and to influence national guidelines on generative AI deployment.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-21www1ev
participants.professional_persona: Urban planner from the Land Transport Authority, working on smart mobility projects. Interested in using AI to optimise bus routes and reduce congestion.
participants.skills_and_expertise: GIS analysis, transport modelling, and basic machine learning with scikit-learn. Has presented at a regional smart city conference.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Become a lead data scientist in a government agency, shaping AI solutions that make Singapore’s transport system more efficient and equitable.

participants.professional_persona: A data scientist who previously worked in financial analytics and retrained via a Career Conversion Programme under TeSA. Now applies natural language processing (NLP) to detect fraud for a local bank.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Strong in statistical modelling, NLP, and responsible AI practices; experienced with Python, R, and cloud ML services.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to become a subject matter expert in AI ethics and governance, and eventually shape policy guidelines for financial AI applications in Singapore.

participants.professional_persona: A mid-career switcher from marketing analytics, now an AI specialist at a government-linked healthcare institution. She builds recommendation systems for personalised patient care pathways.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Well-versed in Python, scikit-learn, and PyTorch; skilled in data storytelling and translating business needs into AI solutions.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Goal is to bridge the gap between clinical practitioners and AI, and to drive adoption of AI tools in public healthcare to improve patient outcomes.

participants.professional_persona: Mr. Sundaram is a self-taught AI researcher who leads the applied ML team at a logistics unicorn. He builds predictive models for last-mile delivery optimization.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Strong in reinforcement learning, time-series forecasting, and operations research; hands-on with MLOps and deploying models at scale.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He wants to transition into a full-time research role and contribute to open-source projects for supply chain AI.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-fv8z8xyd
participants.professional_persona: Freelance graphic designer and illustrator who started using generative AI tools for concept art. Wants to ensure AI augments human creativity rather than replacing artists.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Adobe Creative Suite, Stable Diffusion prompting, and community organising. Runs a small online group for creatives to discuss ethical AI use.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Found a collective that advocates for fair compensation and attribution in AI-generated art, and later develop a certification for ethical AI in creative industries.

participants.professional_persona: A fresh graduate from NUS with a degree in Computer Science, now a junior machine learning engineer at a startup that participated in the 100 Experiments programme. He focuses on deploying Generative AI for customer service automation.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Competent in deep learning frameworks and transformer architectures; familiar with MLOps tools and agile development.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to contribute to cutting-edge research-to-product translation in Singapore, and eventually start his own AI solutions company targeting SMEs.

participants.professional_persona: Dr. Chia is a research fellow at a university medical school, applying AI to genomics and drug discovery. He collaborates with hospitals to predict patient outcomes.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep learning for genomics, graph neural networks for molecular property prediction, and Bayesian optimization.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He aims to bring AI-discovered drug candidates to clinical trials in Singapore and establish a bridge between AI research and clinical practice.

participants.professional_persona: Ms. Lee is an adjunct researcher at SMU and a full-time AI ethics consultant. She advises companies on fairness and transparency in AI systems.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in algorithmic fairness, explainable AI, and AI audit frameworks; practical experience with bias detection and mitigation.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: She wants to help shape Singapore's AI governance policies and create a certification scheme for ethical AI products.

participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-odv8gmog

participants.skills_and_expertise: Process optimisation, troubleshooting complex systems, and patient communication. Has developed simple AI demo modules using Teachable Machine.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Help build a network of senior-friendly AI learning hubs across community centres, and write a guidebook on AI for active ageing.

participants.professional_persona: An experienced AI architect who previously led the AI team of a multinational e-commerce firm. Now works as a CTO-as-a-Service consultant, helping small and medium enterprises (SMEs) adopt AI solutions through the SMEs Go Digital programme.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in cloud-native AI architectures, data engineering, and scalable deployment; strong in stakeholder management and translating business problems into technical roadmaps.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to build a consultancy that specialises in responsible AI adoption for SMEs, and to mentor the next generation of AI practitioners in Singapore.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He aims to build the most accurate speech recognition system for Singlish and regional languages, and to open-source the dataset.

participants.skills_and_expertise: Policy writing, stakeholder consultation, and risk assessment. Holds a graduate certificate in AI ethics from an international programme.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Shape Singapore’s AI governance standards and represent the country at multilateral fora to advance inclusive AI norms.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: He hopes to secure a large grant to build Singapore's first open-source humanoid robot platform and train the next generation of roboticists.
